text.title: Compose Developer + Code Review Flow; Pin Version Floor 18.8
text.summary: Chain Developer Flow into Code Review Flow as the canonical issue-to-merge pipeline, and refuse to enable Duo Agent Platform on GitLab versions below 18.8 because of incident #21171.

section.title: Composition + minimum version
section.p: Developer Flow alone produces an MR but does not gate it; the value of Duo's audit story comes from chaining the second flow so review happens on the same platform with the same audit chain.

rule.statement: The canonical Duo pipeline MUST chain Developer Flow into Code Review Flow: the `agent:implement` label triggers Developer Flow, the resulting MR-opened event triggers Code Review Flow with at least the `security` standard enabled. The project MUST run GitLab 18.8 or later before enabling any Duo flow; pre-18.8 deployments are explicitly out of scope because of the locking bug plus feature-flag misconfiguration documented in GitLab incident #21171. A pre-18.8 detection check MUST run on flow-config load and refuse the run with a "version floor not met" error.
rule.rationale: The composed pipeline is what GitLab's Duo reference architecture targets — Developer alone misses the SAST false-positive filtering that Code Review Flow's `security` standard delivers. The 18.8 floor is non-negotiable: the locking bug in earlier versions causes the Developer Flow to deadlock on MR creation under concurrent label triggers, producing zombie runs the platform cannot clean up. Refusing at config-load time prevents the deadlock from occurring in production.

example.code: flows: developer: trigger: { issue_label_added: "agent:implement" } output: merge_request code_review: trigger: { merge_request_opened: any } standards: [security, perf, style] require_gitlab_version: ">=18.8" # refuse to load on older on_unmet_version: error_and_disable_flows
example.note: Composed pipeline + version-floor check; pre-18.8 fails fast.

example.code: flows: developer: trigger: { issue_label_added: "agent:implement" } output: merge_request # Code Review Flow not chained. # No version floor declared.
example.note: MR is opened without security review and the flow is enabled on pre-18.8 GitLab; concurrent label transitions can deadlock.

antipattern.description: Workaround pre-18.8 incident #21171 by adding a custom CI job that polls MRs every minute.
antipattern.reason: Reintroduces the side-runner anti-pattern; the deadlock symptom is hidden but the audit-trail damage and feature-flag misconfiguration remain. Upgrade GitLab instead.

section.reference: Composed Developer + Code Review Flow config.
Help Net Security: GitLab Duo Agent Platform overview.
